title: Method signatures should avoid containing nested generic types
description: Method signatures should not contain nested generic typesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingWhy is this an issue?A nested type is a type argument that is also a generic type. Calling a method with such a nested type argument requires complicated and confusing code. It should be avoided as much as possible.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; using System.Collections.Generic; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo { public void DoSomething(ICollection<ICollection<int>> outerCollect) // Noncompliant { } } }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

logic.reasoning: This rule detects method signatures with nested generic types (e.g., ICollection<ICollection<int>>), which make method calls complicated and confusing. The pattern matches parameters with nested angle brackets - Type<Type<...>>. This indicates a generic type argument that is itself a generic type. Such nested generics should be avoided by using wrapper types or flattening the structure. The exclusion patterns prevent false positives in logging, testing, and display contexts.
